observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
1. the City Accountant to
a. maintain the PPELCs;
b. undertake collaborative procedures with the CGS Officer to ensure that all PPEs
included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded; and
c. derecognize from the PPE accounts the items costing below ₱15,000.00 totalling
₱2,143,137.80.
2. the Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory count of all PPE items
3. The CGS Officer and the City Accountant, to conduct periodic reconciliation
of the property and accounting records.
1.16 The City Accountant and the CGS Officer committed to comply with the audit
recommendations.

2.19 We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. determine the cause of the negative balance in the Cash-Local Treasury
account and make necessary adjustments;
b. record in the GF books the existing cash balance identified in the DBP account;
c. analyze and verify errors/reconciling items in the current and prior years and
cause the immediate recording of appropriate adjusting entries for the stale checks
and other reconciling items in the books duly supported by documents; and
d. ascertain the cause of the cash deficit incurred in TF amounting to
₱11,647,092.84.
2.20 As to the negative balance in the Cash-Local Treasury account, the City Accountant
explained that the Accounting Department will look into the ledgers to check and trace its
cause. She assured that the DBP account cash balance will be taken up in the books, and
she will coordinate with the Ciy Treasurer pertaining to the stale checks, before effecting
necessary adjustments.
2.21 It was further explained that despite diligent effort of the Accounting staff handling
the TF accounts in identifying and posting correcting entries to clear reconciling items,
material amounts of unidentified reconciling items remained in the TF books due to non-
availability of details and documents, since the balance pertain to CY 2010 and prior years.
2.22 With regard to the cash deficit, the City Accountant commented that her Office has
yet to check on the matter, but this will be included in the planned overall cleansing of all
liability accounts in the TF. She cited that one possible reason might be a failure to debit
liability accounts upon liquidation of funds. The City Accountant, however, assured that
verification of all the entries on each liability account from years 2011 up to 2021 will be
conducted, to trace/determine accounting errors.

3.11 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. conduct a thorough review and analysis of the reciprocal accounts Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to identify the cause of the variance and the
negative balances, and make the adjusting journal entries, if necessary; and
b. henceforth, ensure the proper recording of transfers of funds in the books of
accounts.
3.12 With regard to the inclusion of negative balances, the City Accountant commented
that identifying these transactions would require more time as the documents involved
pertain to CY 2015 and prior years’ transactions. But she committed that the Accounting
Department will conduct a review and start the clean-up of the reciprocal accounts, and
will make necessary adjustments to correct errors and deficiencies in the books after
verification.

4.10 We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. maintain the SLC for each inventory item;
b. periodically reconcile the balance per stock cards of the CGS Office with the SLC
of the Accounting Department; and
c. refrain from taking up/recording inventory in the books without the AIR and its
supporting documents to support delivery.
4.11 The City Accountant committed to comply with the recommendations. SLC will
be maintained and periodic reconciliation of the balance per SLC with the balance per stock
card of the CGSO will be conducted.

5.10 We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
a. determine the causes of the negative (abnormal) balances in the Due to
National Government Agencies account and effect adjusting entries as necessary;
b. conduct a thorough review and analysis of the Due to National Government
Agencies account, so that unaccounted balances could be traced/identified; and
c. reclassify the funds that were not sourced from the NGAs to their proper
account.
5.11 The City Accountant informed that their Office will undertake a clean-up of the
Due to National Government Agencies account, to be able to verify the correctness and
reliability of the balances reflected in the SLs, and detect possible incorrect accounting
entries made.

6.9 We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to
immediately return the unexpended balance of the BGCM from the TF to the GF,
and make the following adjusting entries in the books of accounts upon return of the
fund:
Debit Credit
GF Books
CIB-LCCA ₱1,076,825.49
Trust Liabilities ₱1,076,825.49
TF Books
Due to Local Government Units ₱1,076,825.49
CIB-LCCA ₱1,076,825.49
6.10 The City Accountant responded that the unexpended balance of the BGCM will be
transferred back from the TF to the GF, and will be properly recorded as Trust Laibilities,
as stated in the LBC. She further explained that since the timeline of utilizing the BGCM
has already prescribed, the City will be returning the total unutilized amount of
₱2,813,740.62 to the DBM.

7.13 We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
a. The City Accountant to record proper accounting of the PCF transactions in
the books according to the imprest fund system;
b. The petty cash custodians to:
i. charge against the PCF only the non-recurring and petty operating expenses;
and
ii. fully liquidate the PCF at year end.
7.14 We also recommended that Management properly plan the procurements of
goods to prevent the frequent resort to charging payments of regular and recurring
expenses against the PCF.
7.15 The City Accountant agreed to comply with the recommendations. She committed
that demand letters will be given to petty cash custodians with unliquidated cash advances
on or before December 15, 2022 to ensure liquidation of the PCF at year end.

8.11 We recommended that Management refrain from granting cash advances for
procurements to prevent unnecessary handling of cash.
8.12 Management agreed to comply with the recommendations. The City Accountant
assured strict monitoring and controls on the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

9.8 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the other departments and focal persons of the various SAs to
gather available documents for unsupported dormant fund balances;
b. prepare and submit liquidation reports immediately after verification and
return the unused balances of completed PPAs to the SAs;
c. immediately cause the return of the unexpended PDAF balances to the Bureau
of Treasury; and
d. henceforth, maintain subsidiary records and retain copies of MOA and such
other documents for every fund received for the undertaking of specific purposes.
9.9 The City Accountant informed that the Accounting Department will undertake
cleansing of all financial position accounts, including the Due to National Government
Agencies account, and once they are done with the cleansing of the account and can finally
determine the balances for each fund’s source agencies, they will undertake steps to return
the fund balances. But until full cleansing of the account is done, they will hold the return
of the funds.
9.10 Further, the City Accountant agreed to maintain subsidiary ledgers and retain
copies of MOA and other documents for every fund received. Although, ledgers are
maintained in their system, she admitted that sometimes, said system is not updated.
However, she assured that a separate excel file of subsidiary ledgers will be maintained for
their Office’s proper monitoring and updating of funds received from different source
agencies.

10.13 We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. instruct the City DRRM Officer to submit the monthly report on sources and
utilization on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Local
DRRM Council and Local Development Council to the Audit Team;
b. require the Supply/Property Officer to comply with the submission of the
Inventory List of Items for DRRM, duly supported with Annexes P-1 and P-2, as
required under COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
c. instruct the City Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements the
disclosure requirements on DRRM prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2012-
002 and 2014-002.
10.14 The City Accountant committed to comply with the recommendations. Necessary
disclosure pertaining to DRRM will be included in the Notes to Financial Statements,
separate SLC for the supplies inventory procured from Local DRRM Fund will be
maintained, and monthly reconciliation with the actual balance on hand in CGS Office/City
DRRM Office will be conducted.
10.15 In his reply to the AOM, the CGS Officer informed that he has already coordinated
with the designated Supply Officers of the City DRRM Office to conduct an inventory of
the unused/unissued supplies or property previously issued from stock or directly delivered
to the project site, for submission of inventory reports to COA using the prescribed forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. City Accountant to: (i) coordinate with the CGSO to reconstruct and reconcile
their records, taking into consideration the accuracy of the beginning inventory
balances, the purchases during the year that were recorded as inventory, the
purchases during the year that were recorded as outright expenses, the actual
issuances, and other related adjustments, and prepare the necessary adjusting
entries; (ii) stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials as
outright expense and record expense only upon consumption or distribution;
(iii) coordinate with the Inventory Committee and the CGSO to obtain the balance
of inventory per physical count at the end of the year, reconcile balances and
prepare adjusting entries, if warranted; and (iv) maintain complete supply ledger
cards.

b. CGSO to: (i) maintain complete stock cards; (ii) prepare regularly the RIS and
RSMI with the required information and furnish the City Accountant a copy
thereof for recording; and (iii) reconcile property records with the result of annual
physical count and accounting records;

c. Inventory Committee to: (i) regularly conduct physical count of inventories;
(ii) reconcile the results of the physical count with the accounting and supply
records; and (iii) submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than July
31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to:
(a) reconstruct the Construction in Progress balance by: (i) exerting earnest effort to
trace and identify the unaccounted Construction in Progress balances and the errors
in recording, (ii) reclassifying all completed projects still carried in the Construction
in Progress accounts to the appropriate asset account on the basis of Certificate of
Completion and Acceptance, and (iii) preparing adjusting entries to correct all
deficiencies noted; and (b) henceforth, record payments of projects in their
appropriate Construction in Progress accounts and immediately transfer the same to
their appropriate asset accounts upon submission of Certificate of Completion and
Acceptance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. The City Accountant to ensure that: (a) RPT/SET receivables accruing to the City
are correctly set up in the books based on the amount due and collectible for the
year from the certified list of taxpayers; (b) the RPT and SET discounts availed
by the taxpayers are reflected in the Financial Statements; (c) the accounting
records reconcile with the City Treasurer’s actual collection of RPT and tax
delinquencies; and (d) the necessary adjustments on the noted deficiencies be
immediately effected by strictly adhering to the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.

b. The City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant, at the beginning of each year,
the list of the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the
current year for the recording of the RPT and SET Receivables and of the
corresponding income accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Accountant to make the necessary
adjustments to the affected accounts according to Paragraphs 44 and 45 of IPSAS 23
and to make appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements
regarding the balance of the BGCM Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. The City Accountant to: (i) prepare adjusting entries to correct the error of
recording of Trust Liability-DRRMF in the GF books and to record the transfer
of the unexpended balance of QRF and Local DRRM Fund-MOOE to STF
account in the TF books; (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for the transfer to STF
by year of transfer; and (iii) properly disclose the details of the unexpended
balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

b. The Acting City Treasurer to transfer the unexpended QRF and Local DRRM
Fund – MOOE to the TF bank account.

c. The City DRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of the Local DRRM Fund duly certified correct by the City Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the City immediately comply on the posting and reporting
requirements provided in Section 4 of DBM LBC No. 125 for the utilization of its
BGCM to provide adequate information and disclosure to its constituents.

We recommended that the SP amends the AOs to include the necessary information
in accordance with the provisions of Section 2.3 (c) of the DBM LBC No. 124 dated
March 26, 2020, for transparency.

We recommended, and management agreed, that the Local Chief Executive require
the Acting City Treasurer to verify the OR issued by the contractor/supplier to
acknowledge payment of infrastructure projects/goods/services and ensure that the
gross amount due is indicated in the OR.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The City Accountant, to exert extra effort to determine the unaccounted for prior
years’ balances of ₱5,415,765.00 and initiate, if warranted, a request for write-off of
dormant unliquidated fund transfers for implementation of programs/projects which
remained non-moving for ten years or more and where settlement could no longer be
ascertained pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
b. The project implementers concerned, to use the funds only for their intended
purpose; and
c. The City officials concerned, to review and analyze the MOA of the
unexpended/unimplemented programs/projects and if found that the
implementation of the same is no longer feasible in accordance with the MOA, to
return the fund to the Source Agencies as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013.

1.10 This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance,
as may be warranted.

2.11 We recommended that the City Accountant:
a. Ensure that the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 be strictly
observed, particularly, by requiring the liquidation of cash advances as soon as the
purpose has been served and refraining from granting additional cash advances to
officers and employees if the previous cash advances has not been settled;
b. Take appropriate measures for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances
granted to the City officials/employees.
c. Exert extensive effort to locate relevant documents needed to account for the prior
years’ unaccounted balance;
d. Prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the liquidation of the ₱660,075.60 cash
advances; and
e. Initiate the request for write-off of long outstanding/dormant cash advances with
complete documentary requirements as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

2.12 The OCA recently retrieved the liquidation documents of the ₱660,075.60 cash advances
and agreed with the recommendation of the Audit Team to prepare necessary entries in the
books of accounts.
2.13 As to the prior years’ unaccounted balance, the office concerned claimed that documents
are no longer available; neither can data be extracted from the system since recording was
not yet computerized during that time.
2.14 Likewise, as a preventive measure of accumulating unliquidated cash advances, the OCA
assured that the following is being/will be conducted:
a. Giving monthly demand letters to Employees and Officials with unliquidated cash
advances.
b. On the 3rd demand, Amortization Schedule of deductions on payroll is attached.
Should the employee or official still fail to liquidate after receipt of final notice,
deduction will commence on the next payroll period.
c. OCA will suggest to LCE to pay wages through ATMs to avoid delays in
distribution and liquidations of Advances to Payrolls.

3.8 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the BAC submit APP-CSE in either
printed or electronic copy through an online facility at PS website for the succeeding
years for the common-use supplies needed by the City to be included in the DBM-PS’
projection of inventory requirements.

4.4 We recommended that the City officials concerned adhere to the legal procedures and
documentation on requests for contract time extensions and to the imposition of
liquidated damages on contractors for the incurred delay in the completion of
projects.

4.5 Management agreed with the recommendation of the Audit Team.

4.6 This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance
on the liquidated damages, if warranted.

5.13 We recommended that appropriate signboard should be put-up for projects
undertaken even by administration for information and transparency purposes
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-04.

5.14 We further recommended that materials procured and delivered be submitted for
inspection and acceptance by the GSO; and the delivery receipt and IAR submitted to
COA within 24 hours from acceptance pursuant to Item 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-
02.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Treasurer and City Accountant to review the records of collections and deposits to determine the cause/s of discrepancies and adjust the books accordingly in order to correct the reported balance of the Cash, Local Treasury account for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned city officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Local Chief Executive created Inventory Committee who will conduct inventory of Local Road Networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and her two staff exert more effort to retrieve the supporting documents affecting the payable accounts aging two years and more in order to arrive at fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to reconcile her Loans Payable record with that of the lending institutions so as to present fairly the financial statements. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate the full implementation of all programs/projects/activities in order not to delay further the benefits that could be provided to the intended beneficiaries. observations.managementAction: Out of the 21 projects, only 2 projects are completed during the Year (2017)

observations.recommendation: Management: a) Undertake speedy resolution to cause the immediate completion of the project considering the long period of time that has elapsed from its expiry date as at year-end; b) Institute a monitoring system in the City Engineer’s Office for all projects so that immediate action can be undertaken on lapsed projects; c) Impose liquidated damages for every day of delay or file appropriate legal action against the contractor, if warranted; and d) To blacklist the contractor from future projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: Construction of Slaughterhouse was implemented and completed on August 23, 2019 with approved extension. City Engineer instituted monitoring and recommended imposition of liquidated damages and other actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (a) Order the Heads of the Business Permits and Licensing Office and Office of the City Public Market and Slaughterhouse to coordinate with each other by providing the former the list of stall awardees, likewise, furnishing the latter the list of official business registrants, on a regular basis, for a timely and appropriate action on both ends; (b) The BPLO to conduct quarterly tax mapping; and (c) To revisit the process of acquiring Mayor’s Permit from the Business Permits and Licensing Office so as to correct this irregular practice of the twenty-nine (29) stall occupants of evading their payment of said permit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management intensify and expand the coverage of current practice of publicizing vacancy or implement different strategies of reaching prospective lessees with the aid of modern technology and social media, in order to accumulate additional revenue which could be used to meet its obligations or implement other beneficial projects or programs.
observations.managementAction: Market office posted ads in conspicuous places and came up with different social media strategies of reaching prospective concessionaires with Information page of City

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Fast-track the bidding and procurement process of the projects, and ensure proper monitoring of on-going and lapsed projects to provide benefits for the constituents within the timeframe defined in the Annual Investment Program; and conduct monitoring. b) Put up signboards on the project premises or location to promote transparency and ensure accountability.
observations.managementAction: The City implemented some projects; the BAC to expedite procurement and monitoring; signboards posted in some projects.
observations.reason: Some projects implemented; 3 projects lacking documents; delayed compliance with posting requirements

observations.recommendation: Management: a. Direct all JO employees to comply strictly with Section 1 to 5 of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and to report for work in the Office from eight o’clock to twelve o’clock in the morning and from one o’clock to five o’clock in the afternoon from Monday to Friday, in order to ensure that the amount of wages paid by the government is based on the services they actually rendered and to prevent any doubt of ghost employees. b. Instruct the City CHRMO to strengthen the internal control system in the hiring of additional workforce through job/order/contract of service. Limit the JO hiring to a maximum of three (3) per Office or to actual number needed for normal operations taking into consideration the volume of transactions per Office. Moreover, to stop hiring students and senior citizens who might not be able to satisfactorily perform the work they were hired for.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare the journal entry vouchers to effect transfer of completed projects from Construction in Progress account to its appropriate asset account, and to record all reconciling items and adjustments. Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the City from the occurrence of natural calamities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor require the management to strictly comply with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 and ensure that the programs, projects and activities that are included in its plan are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that the GAD provisions seeks to provide.

observations.reason: Allocations revised and separated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to withhold taxes from the honoraria claims of the BAC members, secretariat, technical working group, health workers and other officials and employees who render services beyond their regular workload in compliance to R.A. 8424.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office ensures that honoraria received by BAC members are included in their Taxable Income for proper calculation of Income Tax and withholdings.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy between the balance per books and the balance in the bank statement of the General Fund account. Moreover, prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the various reconciling items and to reflect the correct amount in the Cash in Bank account of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation prepared from CY 2015 onwards for all funds and subsdiary ledgers maintained for all funds; inactive bank accounts closed.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers are based on GAAMS system but account codes are not updated so use of new system is pending

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances that has long been outstanding in the books. Moreover, to avail of all administrative and legal remedies to collect the unsettled advances from concerned officers and employees whether in service or already out of service. Also, City Accounting Office to automatically deduct amortization of unliquidated cash advances of employees and officials who have no actions after receiving 3 demand letters already.
observations.managementAction: City Accounting Office sends demand letters to the employees and officials with unliquidated cash advances; on 3rd demand amortization schedule attached and deductions made; Ongoing investigation and adjustments in process.
observations.reason: On-going investigation of the discrepancy between Aging and Trial Balance; deductions being implemented; some amounts deducted from terminal leave benefits

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management closely monitor the implementation and completion of the projects within the specified timeframe as these were commitments of the LGU during the Year (2017) so as not to delay or further deprive the constituents of the benefits arising from these socio-economic development and environmental projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; some projects completed; management continuing to monitor and expedite implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of timely bidding and procurement and other delays; only some projects completed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the information and publicity requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and to include projects undertaken by administration to achieve full disclosure, transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented with posting of some signboards and public notices; management to ensure compliance in future.
observations.reason: Signboards posted in several projects; compliance ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management fast track and monitor the implementation and/or enforce the completion of the project in order to achieve optimum utilization of funds allocated for the purpose and to avail of the benefits derived from the immediate full use of the sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: Phase I completed; Phase II bidding underway; City implementing measures to proceed with Phase II and Phase III upon securing required permits.
observations.reason: Phase II bidding and required permits pending; some activities completed

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive pursuant to Section 455(x) of R.A. 7160 should cause the compliance of Section 74, P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. Direct the City Accountant to: 1) prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all existing bank accounts; 2) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for all existing bank accounts; 3) close the inactive accounts with DBP and deposit it to another existing bank account after verification is made to avoid incurrence of service charges from the bank and; 4) submit copies of the monthly BRS to the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Bank reconciliations prepared from CY 2015 onwards for all funds; City Treasurer closed inactive bank accounts on CY 2017 and transferred balances; subsidiary ledgers being maintained in GAAMS.
observations.reason: Subsi diary ledgers are based on GAAMS system but system codes not fully updated; new system pending full implementation

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to trace the entries in the general ledger to the journals and to its source documents to establish the cause of the disparity of the amounts recorded in both records, and adjust the books accordingly. Likewise, require the City Accountant to submit a Schedule of Aging of Receivables for Cash Disbursing Officer, Payroll Fund, and Advances to Officers and Employees, otherwise, enforce Section 122(2) of PD 1445, which provides for the automatic suspension of payment of Accountant’s salary until she is able to comply with the requirements of COA.
observations.managementAction: The Cash Disbursing Officer, Payroll Fund and Advances to Officers and Employees are now being monitored and necessary entries posted; reconciliation performed and adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Accountant and Assessor to maintain ledger cards and property cards respectively, in accordance with Section 119 and 120 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I. Likewise, the properties should be registered in the name of the City pursuant to the provisions of Section 23(2), Title I-B Book V of the 1987 Revised Administrative Code.
observations.managementAction: City begun clean-up of records; Assessor coordinating with Land Registration to secure titles; CGSO to maintain property cards for PPEs; ledger cards maintained from 2017 onwards.
observations.reason: Process of securing titles and clean-up of records is ongoing; some PPEs from prior years not yet traced

observations.recommendation:
1.12 To fast track reconciliation of the PPE account balances per books and per
RPCPPE; to maintain and update the PLCs and PCs; and to effect the necessary
adjustments, we recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor
direct:

c. The City General Services Officer –
1. To coordinate with different offices of the city to secure the
following: (a) management’s titles over real properties; (b) available
prior year’s PPE records in their file that would be of help in the
completion of the property cards;
2. To make an appraisal of the property should be made to come up
with the current value thereof, if records could no longer be
retrieved;
3. To demand from accountable employees the presentment or
production of the lost/stolen/missing equipment or the payment of
the equivalent money value of the same, otherwise, appropriate
action should be taken in line with Section 105 of PD No. 1445.

d. The City Accountant, to exert extra effort to locate and extract lost records
of PPE to complete the PLCs to serve as alternative basis to check the
accuracy of the inventory with the account balances in the General Ledger,
in compliance with Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual on NGAS,
Volume I.

2.8 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the city officials concerned to strictly follow the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road
and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly recorded and
valued for the fair presentation of the Road Networks account in the financial
statements.

3.10 We recommended and management agreed that the City Accountant
prioritize the reconciliation of intra-agency accounts to adjust/correct the
current variance of ₱27,698,028.65 and effect the necessary correcting entries
immediately in order to present fairly the financial statements.

4.5 We recommended and management agreed that the City Accountant and her
staff retrieve the supporting documents; settle the payables aging two years and
more; and revert to surplus those accounts that could not be settled.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed that the City Accountant provide
CY 2018 depreciation for the infrastructure projects completed during CY 2017
and prepare necessary journal entries to present fairly all affected accounts in the
financial statements. We also recommended that the City Accountant be more
scrupulous in recording succeeding depreciation to avoid similar errors in the
future.

6.7 In addition, we recommended that engineering department furnish the Office
of the City Accountant the monthly status report of all infrastructure projects
which could help address erroneous computation of depreciation expense.

7.8 We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor require the City
Accountant to reconcile the Loans Payable-Domestic account with that of the
lending institutions and make the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the
Financial Statements pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

8.7 We recommended and management agreed that the CGSO furnish the Office
of the City Accountant, the disposal documents of the unserviceable
properties for timely recording. We also recommended that the Office of the
City Accountant prepare journal entries for the correction of the PPE and
Government Equity balances.

9.6 We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor direct the OIC-
City Treasurer and the City Accountant to review the records of collections and
deposits to determine the cause/s of discrepancies and adjust the books
accordingly in order to correct the reported balance of the Cash-Local Treasury
account for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

10.4 We recommended and management agreed to fast track completion of
partially and unimplemented BuB programs/projects/activities in order to
provide the intended benefits to the beneficiaries. Moreover, impose
liquidated damages for unwarranted delays.

11.11 We recommended and management agreed to:

e) Undertake the immediate completion of the project considering the time
that has elapsed from its expiry date as at year-end;
f) Institute a monitoring system in the City Engineer’s Office for all projects
so that immediate action can be undertaken as regards lapsed projects;
g) Impose liquidated damages for every day of delay or file appropriate legal
action against the contractor, if warranted; and
h) To blacklist the contractor from future projects of the City.

12.7 We recommended and management agreed that management (a) Order the Heads
of the BPLO and OCPMS to coordinate by providing and furnishing each other
the necessary documents on a regular basis, for a timely and appropriate actions
by both offices; (b) Order the BPLO to conduct a quarterly tax mapping or as
often as may be necessary; and (c) Strictly follow the procedure of requiring the
Mayor’s Permit from the stall awardees immediately after the award.

13.7 We recommended and management agreed to intensify and expand
information dissemination and publication of stall vacancies or to come up
with different strategies of reaching prospective lessees with the aid of modern
technology and social media, in order to attract prospective lessees and to
generate additional revenue which could be used to implement other
beneficial projects or programs.

14.10 We recommended and management agreed to:

c) Fast-track the bidding and procurement process of the ₱58,000,000.00
unimplemented development projects and ensure proper monitoring of
on-going and lapsed projects for the timely completion of the same; and
d) Put up the required signboards on the project premises or location to
promote transparency and accountability.

15.11 We recommended and management agreed to:

c. Direct all JO employees to comply strictly with Sections 1 to 5 of CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and to require them to time in and out
using the biometrics machine as a control measure and to report for work in
the Office from eight o’clock to twelve o’clock in the morning and from one
o’clock to five o’clock in the afternoon from Monday to Friday, in order to
ensure that the amount of wages paid by the government is based on the
services they actually render and to prevent any doubt of ghost employees.
d. Instruct the City Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen the
internal control system in hiring of additional workforce through
job/order/contract of service. Limit the hiring of JOs to what is necessary or
to actual number needed for normal operations taking into consideration the
volume of transactions per Office.

16.9 We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor require the City
officials concerned to comply immediately with the requirements of the composite
team in order to secure the SLUP so that succeeding phases of the project will
commence to achieve maximum utilization of funds allocated for it and to avail of
the consequent benefits from the immediate full use of the sanitary landfill.

17.10 We recommended and management agreed that the City stop the unnecessary
hiring of barangay coordinators in every barangay for the collection and
segregation of garbage and conduct continuous IEC in barangays to heighten the
awareness of the City’s constituents on proper waste management.

18.16 It is recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor:
1. Instruct the LDRRMO to include all unexpended prior years’ balances in
its LDRRMF Investment Plan;
2. Instruct the City Accountant to transfer all unexpended balances of
LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM
account and to follow the suggested format in the Notes to Financial
Statement for DRRMF at year end;
3. Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to effectively increase
the level of preparedness of the City during natural calamities.

19.8 We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned officials to strictly
comply with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 and ensure
that the programs, projects and activities that are included in its plan are aligned
with GAD-related undertakings to maximize its benefits and to account separately
the projects for the LCPC.

20.4 The City Accountant replied that as required by the AOM and in compliance to RA
No. 8424 or the Tax Reform Act of 1997, she will require the Payroll Section of the
City Accounting Office to observe and include the following as taxable income for
CY 2019:

a. BAC honoraria;
b. MCP and Philhealth honoraria of the health workers; and
c. PSB deliberation honoraria.

We recommended and management agreed that the City
Mayor direct the City Accountant to reconcile the P2,646,222.67 discrepancy between the balance per
books and the balance in the bank statement of the General Fund Cash in Bank
account; and to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the various
reconciling items and to reflect the correct amount in the Cash in Bank account of
the Financial Statements.

We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant to strictly enforce the laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization
and liquidation of cash advances; to reconcile the aging schedule with the recorded
balance in the General Fund; and to prepare the aging schedule of the SEF and Trust
Fund.

Further, we recommend that the City Mayor require all employees with outstanding
cash advances as of December 31, 2017, to settle/liquidate their cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor direct:
1. The City Budget Officer to stop obligating expenses without supporting
documents; and
2. The City Accountant,
a. to stop taking up as Accounts Payable those procurements of construction
materials and hardware supplies of projects undertaken by administration
without complete documentation;
b. to submit within five days the required documents to support the payments
of these accounts payable.

We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor direct the City
Engineer to closely monitor the implementation and completion of the projects
within the specified time frame as these were commitments of the City during the
Year (2017) and so as not to delay or deprive the constituents of the benefits
emanating from these socio-economic development and environmental projects.

We recommended and management agreed that the City institute measures in place
for procurement of goods for distribution to barangays and schools, and direct:
1. The City Accountant,
a. to refrain from immediately charging the procurement to Expense
account without the report from the General Service Officer of issuances.
b. to properly monitor the recipients of materials donated to the schools
from different sources to avoid duplication.
c. to require the submission of a Sangguniang Barangay Resolution
authorizing the Punong Barangay to negotiate and secure financial
assistance or donation in kind with Pre-Inspection Report of works to be
undertaken and Program of Work/Bill of Materials.
2. The CGSO, to properly fill up an RIS and with acknowledgement receipt
signed by the beneficiary as evidence that the goods purchased were actually
received.

We recommended and management agreed that the City Mayor require the BAC
to strictly follow the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts and
other supporting documents within five days from execution thereof pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended and management agreed that the City will strictly comply with
the information and publicity requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-
004, to include all projects undertaken by administration to achieve full disclosure,
transparency and accountability.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. the City Accountant to prepare regularly the monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements for all funds and maintain Subsidiary
Ledgers for them.

b. the City Treasurer to close the four (4) dormant accounts and transfer
them to the regular Trust Fund account maintained by the City to
avoid incurring unnecessary bank charges, and

c. the City Accountant to set-up Subsidiary Ledger for each of the
transferred account so as to keep track of the origin and purpose of the
fund.

We recommend that the City Mayor:

a. relieve the Accountable Officer from her duties as collecting officer

b. compel the concerned Accountable Officer to fully restitute the total unremitted
collections of P429,930.25 and to immediately produce the remaining missing
receipts;

c. upon failure to restitute her money accountability, withhold the payment of salary
and other emoluments due her;

d. require the Liquidating Officer to check the statement of accountable forms of
the remitting collector as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the
ending balances on hand. Also, to make a physical count of the accountable forms
remaining in the custody of the collector and check the same against the new
balances on hand column and indicate her verification by affixing her signature at
the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued;

e. require the person-in-charge of issuance of accountable forms to stop the practice
of issuing additional accountable forms unless previous issuances have all been
duly accounted for;

f. strengthen internal control in the collecting system of the City such that no
collector will accept payment of taxes and licenses without the tax order payment
from the BPLO, and copy of said tax order payment together with the duplicate
copies of the issued receipt forming part of the report submitted for audit. Moreover,
cause the monthly reconciliation between the report of the BPLO and the
collection report of the Treasurer’s Office and to investigate differences, if any;

g. institute appropriate administrative and or criminal case against the
erring accountable officer for the misuse and/or misappropriation of said
collections.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the City
Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to effect transfer of
completed projects from the Construction in Progress account to its
appropriate asset account, pursuant to Section 50 of NGAS Manual for
Local Government Units.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee should strengthen
its bid post-qualification procedures in the government procurements of
Infrastructure Projects, taking considerations on the technical and
financial capabilities of the prospective contractors to prosecute the
contracts that are advantageous and favorable to the government and the
public to maintain credibility of its procurement process. Moreover, to
subject contractors submitting eligibility documents containing false
information to penalties under Section 65 of the Revised IRR.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the City Engineer
to ensure the immediate completion of the delayed projects and review
thoroughly future program of work and evaluate project design and
estimates to avoid time extensions and be able to effectively provide the
public the intended benefits of the project. If warranted, impose liquidated
damages or even rescind the contract as provided in Section 68 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the City Accountant comply with the provision of
PPSAS 17 on the recognition principle of Property, Plant and Equipment
to avoid overstatement of the account for a fair presentation of the
financial statement.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the information and
publicity requirements under COA Circular No. 2013-004 particularly on the
design and format of the tarpaulin posted at the project location.

We recommend that the City Mayor immediately put up counterpart fund as
agreed so that implementation of the Barangays Banuyo and Benowangan Health
Stations facility enhancement will not be further delayed. Further, for the BuB
focal person to coordinate with DTI on how best to pursue the Ice Block Production
project considering the discrepancy between the LGU proposal and the agency’s
guideline on its implementation. Moreover, for the Local Poverty Reduction Action
Team (LPRAT) to intensify the monitoring of the implementation of all BuB
projects so as not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the benefits that can be
derived therefrom.

We recommend that management stop the deployment of job order personnel to
the barangays to avoid duplication of functions and wastage of government
resources. Moreover, to revisit the organizational structure and staffing pattern of
the City and determine the appropriate manpower complement of both the
executive and legislative offices in order to regulate the hiring of casuals and job
order personnel. Further, maximize the services of regular and casual employees
and hire job order personnel only when extremely necessary.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and other personnel concerned
follow the guidelines set forth in Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015
on the disbursement of Intelligence Expenses.

We recommend that the City School Board should make sure that future Budget
allocations conform to pertinent provisions of RA 7160, DECS guidelines and other
existing rules and regulations in order to avoid audit suspensions and
disallowances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive compel the City Accountant to
submit the financial statements together with the schedules to the Audit Team for
verification purposes within the deadline set by existing regulations. Moreover,
institute measures within the Accounting Section so that this problem will not
recur.
